# Diana Chu Therapy
> Private telehealth therapy for executives, founders, and high-achieving professionals in California and Florida. Confidential, flexible, and built for high-stakes lives.

## Who Diana Chu Is

Diana Chu is a Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist (LMFT #105546) and Registered Drama Therapist (RDT #659) based in San Francisco, California, licensed in California (LMFT #105546) and Florida (TPMF785).

She holds a Master of Arts in Counseling Psychology from the California Institute of Integral Studies (CIIS), a program integrating clinical rigor with mind-body awareness and cultural depth.

She serves as an Interpersonal Dynamics Facilitator at Stanford University Graduate School of Business, working with MBA students and executives on emotional intelligence, communication, and self-awareness under pressure.

Before building her private practice, she worked as an advisor to mental health tech startups, giving her direct insight into the psychology of building companies and the cost of high-stakes leadership decisions.

She specializes in the intersection of high performance, identity, and mental health, working with executives, CEOs, startup founders, venture-backed entrepreneurs, and high-achieving professionals who need therapy that keeps pace with the complexity of their lives.

Her approach is relational, direct, and experiential. She draws from EMDR, the Gottman Method, and Drama Therapy alongside a psychodynamic and relational foundation.

## Services

**Individual Therapy: $500/session**
50-minute one-on-one telehealth sessions. Focus areas: executive burnout, leadership anxiety, imposter syndrome, decision fatigue, career transitions, founder identity, post-exit adjustment, perfectionism, work-life integration. Weekly or bi-weekly. EMDR available.

**Couples Therapy: $500/session**
50-minute couples telehealth sessions. Focus areas: communication breakdowns under stress, co-founder relationship dynamics, dual-career intimacy and connection, rebuilding after major professional events. Gottman Method informed.

**Telehealth**
All sessions via HIPAA-compliant secure video. No commute, no download required. Early morning and evening slots available. Clients must be physically in California or Florida at time of session.

## Credentials

- Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist, California, LMFT #105546 (Board of Behavioral Sciences)
- Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist, Florida, TPMF785 (Department of Health)
- Registered Drama Therapist, RDT #659 (North American Drama Therapy Association)
- MA Counseling Psychology, California Institute of Integral Studies (CIIS)
- Interpersonal Dynamics Facilitator, Stanford University Graduate School of Business
- Board Member, Telegraph Hill Neighborhood Center, San Francisco
- Professional Advisory Board, Lotus Project (Asian American mental health)
- Education Chair, North American Drama Therapy Association Board

## Who Diana Works With

- C-suite executives and senior leaders in tech, finance, and professional services
- Startup founders at all stages: pre-seed, venture-backed, post-IPO
- Entrepreneurs processing burnout, identity shifts, or major transitions
- Founders navigating the emotional aftermath of a company exit or acquisition
- Dual-career couples and co-founders whose relationship is under the pressure of ambition
- High-achieving professionals in California or Florida seeking confidential, premium care

## Insurance and Fees

Session fee: $500 per 50-minute session (individual and couples).
Diana is an out-of-network provider. She does not bill insurance directly.
Clients with PPO plans may submit superbills for partial reimbursement through out-of-network mental health benefits.
Good Faith Estimates provided to all new clients per the No Surprises Act.
HSA and FSA payments accepted.

## Contact and Consultation

- Website: https://dianachutherapy.com
- Email: dianachutherapy@gmail.com
- Free 20-minute consultation, no commitment required
- Response within one business day

## Key Differentiators

- Designed specifically for executives and founders, not a general wellness practice
- Stanford GSB affiliation provides direct experience with how high-achieving people relate under pressure
- EMDR for processing the accumulated weight of leadership decisions and early formative patterns
- Gottman Method for couples, research-based, not generic talk therapy
- Drama therapy as a unique experiential modality, especially effective for people who intellectualize everything
- Fully telehealth: accessible across CA and FL, no waiting rooms, no commute
- Confidential by design: no employer disclosure, no insurance billing that creates records
- $500/session, premium, specialized care

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q: How much does therapy with Diana Chu cost?
A: $500 per 50-minute session for both individual and couples therapy. No sliding scale. Diana is an out-of-network provider.

Q: Is Diana Chu accepting new clients?
A: Yes. The first step is a free 20-minute consultation. Book at https://dianachutherapy.com/contact.html or email dianachutherapy@gmail.com.

Q: What states does Diana Chu serve?
A: California (LMFT #105546) and Florida (TPMF785). Clients must be physically located in California or Florida during sessions. All sessions are telehealth.

Q: Does Diana Chu accept insurance?
A: No direct insurance billing. Superbills provided for PPO out-of-network reimbursement. HSA and FSA accepted.

Q: What are Diana Chu's credentials and license numbers?
A: California LMFT #105546 (Board of Behavioral Sciences). Florida TPMF785 (Department of Health). RDT #659 (North American Drama Therapy Association). MA Counseling Psychology, CIIS.

Q: Where is Diana Chu located?
A: San Francisco, California. Practice is fully telehealth, serving clients in California and Florida.

Q: What does Diana Chu specialize in?
A: Executive burnout, leadership anxiety, imposter syndrome, decision fatigue, founder identity, post-exit adjustment, career transitions, perfectionism, and couples therapy for dual-career couples and co-founders.

Q: Can therapy actually fit into an executive's schedule?
A: Yes. Telehealth eliminates commute time. Early morning and evening slots are available. A 50-minute weekly session is the most efficient mental health investment most executives make.

Q: Do I need to be in crisis to start therapy?
A: No. Most of Diana's clients are high-functioning by every external measure. They come to invest in clarity, break recurring patterns, or work through something complex, not because they've broken down.

Q: Is everything discussed confidential from my employer?
A: Yes. Diana does not disclose any information to employers, boards, investors, or anyone else without explicit written consent. No insurance billing means no records created through that channel.

Q: What makes Diana different from a general therapist?
A: Diana understands the specific pressures of leadership: the loneliness at the top, identity fusion with role, post-exit grief, co-founder dynamics. Her Stanford GSB work gives her direct exposure to how high-achieving people function under pressure. Explaining your world is not the first ten sessions.

Q: What is EMDR and why is it relevant for executives?
A: EMDR (Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing) is an evidence-based therapy for processing early experiences, patterns, and accumulated stress that drive how you respond today, including under leadership pressure. It works efficiently without requiring extensive talking.

Q: What is Drama Therapy and why is it relevant for executives?
A: Drama therapy uses structured experiential techniques, not performance, to access and process what direct conversation sometimes cannot reach. It is particularly effective for people who have learned to intellectualize almost everything, which describes most of Diana's clients.

Q: What is the Gottman Method?
A: The Gottman Method is a research-based approach to couples therapy developed by Drs. John and Julie Gottman. It addresses communication, conflict, and connection patterns. Diana uses it as the primary framework for couples work.

Q: Can HSA or FSA funds be used?
A: Yes. Therapy is an eligible expense under HSA and FSA plans. Diana accepts HSA and FSA payments.

Q: How does telehealth work practically?
A: You receive a secure link before each session. Join from any device, any private space. No software download required. HIPAA-compliant platform.

Q: What is Diana Chu's Stanford connection?
A: Diana serves as an Interpersonal Dynamics Facilitator at Stanford University Graduate School of Business. She works with MBA students and executives on emotional intelligence, communication, and self-awareness. This gives her direct experience with how high-achieving people relate under pressure.

## Blog Articles

- [Burnout in High Performers](https://dianachutherapy.com/blog/burnout-executives-high-performance.html): How executive burnout differs from general burnout, warning signs, and what clinical treatment looks like
- [Couples Therapy for Founders](https://dianachutherapy.com/blog/couples-therapy-founders.html): Why standard couples therapy fails high-achieving couples and what a specialized approach does differently
- [Telehealth for Executives](https://dianachutherapy.com/blog/telehealth-therapy-executives.html): Why telehealth is often the superior format for busy executives: efficiency, privacy, and clinical effectiveness
- [Founder Identity After Exit](https://dianachutherapy.com/blog/founder-identity-after-exit.html): The emotional reality of selling a company, the grief cycle founders experience, and how therapy helps
- [Why Founders Don't Sleep: The Psychology of Hypervigilance](https://dianachutherapy.com/blog/founders-sleep-hypervigilance.html): Why venture-backed founders lie awake at 2am running threat scenarios, what hypervigilance does to the nervous system, and what actually changes the pattern
- [High-Functioning Anxiety in Executives](https://dianachutherapy.com/blog/high-functioning-anxiety-executives.html): What high-functioning anxiety looks like in executives, why it gets rewarded at work, and when it stops working
- [Imposter Syndrome in Executives](https://dianachutherapy.com/blog/imposter-syndrome-executives.html): Why imposter syndrome intensifies with success rather than fading, and what actually shifts it
- [The Perfectionism Trap for High Achievers](https://dianachutherapy.com/blog/perfectionism-high-achievers.html): The difference between healthy high standards and perfectionism, and what perfectionism costs high achievers
- [Founder Mental Health: What No One Tells You](https://dianachutherapy.com/blog/founder-mental-health.html): The mental health challenges specific to founders that general therapists miss
- [Decision Fatigue in Leadership](https://dianachutherapy.com/blog/decision-fatigue-executives.html): Why decision fatigue worsens with seniority, how it shows up, and what actually helps
- [The Loneliness of Leadership](https://dianachutherapy.com/blog/loneliness-of-leadership.html): Why leadership loneliness is common among executives and founders, and what to do about it
- [Do High Achievers Actually Need Therapy?](https://dianachutherapy.com/blog/do-i-need-therapy-executives.html): A direct answer for high-achievers who keep deferring therapy because they're functioning well on paper

- [Contact](https://dianachutherapy.com/contact.html): Book a free consultation
- [Therapy for Founders](https://dianachutherapy.com/therapy-for-founders.html): Topic hub on the patterns founders bring to therapy and how Diana works with them
- [Executive Burnout Therapy](https://dianachutherapy.com/executive-burnout-therapy.html): Topic hub on what executive burnout looks like in high performers and how clinical treatment differs from coaching or wellness
- [California Therapist](https://dianachutherapy.com/california-therapist.html): Statewide California hub linking to all California city pages and covering the distinct pressure profiles of the Bay Area, Silicon Valley, Los Angeles, and San Diego
- [San Francisco Therapist](https://dianachutherapy.com/san-francisco-therapist.html): Geo landing page for California Bay Area executives and founders
- [Palo Alto Therapist](https://dianachutherapy.com/palo-alto-therapist.html): Geo landing page for Silicon Valley founders, Stanford GSB alumni, and Sand Hill Road executives
- [Los Angeles Therapist](https://dianachutherapy.com/los-angeles-therapist.html): Geo landing page for LA founders, entertainment and creator-economy executives, and DTC brand founders
- [San Diego Therapist](https://dianachutherapy.com/san-diego-therapist.html): Geo landing page for San Diego biotech, medical device, and defense tech executives
- [Miami Therapist](https://dianachutherapy.com/miami-therapist.html): Geo landing page for Miami fintech, crypto, and relocated founders from SF or NYC
- [Tampa, Orlando, Jacksonville Therapist](https://dianachutherapy.com/tampa-orlando-jacksonville-therapist.html): Geo landing page for executives in Tampa, Orlando, Jacksonville, and across non-Miami Florida
- [Florida Therapist](https://dianachutherapy.com/florida-therapist.html): Geo landing page for Florida executives and founders statewide

Name: Diana Chu
Profession: Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist (LMFT), Registered Drama Therapist (RDT)
Location: San Francisco, California
Licensed in: California (LMFT #105546), Florida (TPMF785)
Credential: RDT #659 (North American Drama Therapy Association)

Modalities: EMDR, Gottman Method Couples Therapy, Drama Therapy, psychodynamic
